  SETPC :C1
  REPEAT 18 [

    LT 10
    SETPC :C1
  ]
  SETPC 15
  REPEAT 6 [

    RT 60
  ]
END

TO RING :N :R :L :A :C1 :C2
  REPEAT :N [
    PETAL :L :A :C1 :C2
    RT 360/:N
    PU
    FD :R
    PD
    BK :R
  ]
END

TO STARSPINE :R :C
  SETPC :C
  REPEAT 8 [
    ARC 8 180
    RT 45
    ARC 4 180
    LT 45
  ]
END

TO LACE :R :N :S
  PU
  FD :R
  PD
  REPEAT :N [
    STARSPINE :S 14
    RT 360/:N
  ]
  PU
  BK :R
  PD
END

TO FRACTALWHEEL :R :N :D :C1 :C2
  IF :D = 0 [STOP]
  REPEAT :N [
    SETPC :C1
    CIRCLE :R
    RT 360/:N
    PU
    FD :R * 2
    PD
    FRACTALWHEEL :R * 0.45 :N :D - 1 :C2 :C1
    PU
    BK :R * 2
    PD
  ]
END

SETBG 15
SETPW 1

TO LEAFSPIRAL :LEN :ANG :SHR :DEPTH
IF :DEPTH = 0 [STOP]
REPEAT 2 [
  SETPC 30 + :DEPTH
  CIRCLE :LEN
  RT :ANG
  FD :LEN / 6
  LT :ANG / 2
]
PU
FD :LEN / 4
PD
RT :ANG
FD :LEN
LT :ANG / 3
LEAFSPIRAL :LEN * :SHR :ANG * 1.03 :SHR :DEPTH - 1
LT :ANG / 2
FD :LEN / 8
RT :ANG
LEAFSPIRAL :LEN * :SHR :ANG * 0.98 :SHR :DEPTH - 1
BK :LEN / 8
LT :ANG / 3
END

TO VINE :LEN :ANG :SHR :DEPTH
IF :DEPTH = 0 [STOP]
SETPC 50 + :DEPTH
ARC :LEN * 1.2 :ANG
FD :LEN / 2
RT :ANG / 2
ARC :LEN :ANG / 1.5
LT :ANG / 3
FD :LEN / 3
VINE :LEN * :SHR :ANG * 1.04 :SHR :DEPTH - 1
PU
BK :LEN / 3
PD
RT :ANG / 2
FD :LEN / 4
LT :ANG / 2
END

TO PETAL :R :A
REPEAT 2 [
  ARC :R :A
  RT 180 - :A
]
END

TO BLOSSOM :R :N
IF :N = 0 [STOP]
SETPC 60 + :N
PETAL :R :360 / :N
RT 360 / :N
BLOSSOM :R * 0.93 :N - 1
END

TO STARSEED :R
REPEAT 12 [
  SETPC 90 + REPCOUNT
  FD :R
  BK :R
  RT 30
]
END

TASK: Generate the requested number of visually distinct variants that are more aesthetically interesting.

INPUTS (in the user message):
- Aesthetic goal: user's taste preference
- Change magnitude: how drastically to evolve
- Base program: Logo code to evolve
- Critic feedback (if iterating): suggestions from previous round

OUTPUT FORMAT - CRITICAL: return ONLY variant blocks:
---VARIANT 1---
[complete Logo code]
---VARIANT 2---
[complete Logo code]

MAGNITUDE RULE: The STRICT MAGNITUDE CONSTRAINT in the user message is absolute. Magnitude 1: change exactly one number. Magnitude 2: change 2-3 numbers. Magnitude 3: change several parameters or add one simple command. Never add new procedures or structural changes at magnitudes 1-3.

COMPLETENESS RULE: Every variant MUST be a complete, runnable Logo program. Verify: (1) every [ has a matching ]; (2) every TO has a matching END; (3) the last line is complete. Do NOT include ANY reasoning, thinking, or commentary inside variant blocks.

Commands: FD BK RT LT PU PD HOME CS REPEAT n [ ] TO name :p (body) END MAKE "var val :var SETHEADING SETXY SETPC SETPENCOLOR SETPENSIZE SETWIDTH SETBG ARC CIRCLE DOT IF IFELSE WHILE FOR SIN COS SQRT RANDOM INT ABS MOD MAX MIN REPCOUNT OUTPUT STOP

SETPC/SETBG: number 0-15, named color (RED BLUE GREEN CYAN MAGENTA YELLOW WHITE BLACK ORANGE PURPLE GOLD TEAL LIME VIOLET INDIGO CRIMSON CORAL SKYBLUE MINT ROSE AMBER GRAY SILVER MAROON OLIVE), or [r g b] 0-255.
Canvas 400x400, origin center, North=up. FINITE programs. TO...END before calling. Under 60 lines.
apiLog.userPrompt:
Your aesthetic goal: "Organic and flowing — curves and spirals that suggest natural growth"
STRICT MAGNITUDE CONSTRAINT — this overrides everything else: Moderate — noticeable evolution; the original structure is still recognisable.
Even if critic feedback suggests larger changes, the magnitude constraint is absolute.
Critic feedback to incorporate: "For the next iteration, make the spiral loosen gradually by increasing the radius a little on each turn and slightly varying the turning angle so the curve feels more like a growing vine than a perfect loop."


Base program to evolve:
REPEAT 180 [FD 1.7 RT 6 FD 1 LT 2 RT 0.15 FD 0.2 RT (SIN REPCOUNT)]
REPEAT 110 [FD 1.45 RT 6.5 FD 0.9 LT 1.8 RT 0.11 FD 0.16 RT (SIN REPCOUNT)]
REPEAT 80 [FD 0.95 RT 8.5 FD 0.5 LT 1.1 RT 0.09 FD 0.1 RT (SIN REPCOUNT)]

Edit budget: moderate restructure — variants exceeding this budget are wrong.
Generate 2 DISTINCT variant(s). Format exactly as:
---VARIANT 1---
[Logo code]
---VARIANT 2---
[Logo code]
(continue for all 2 variants)

Each variant must be meaningfully different within the magnitude constraint. Return ONLY the variant blocks.

REPEAT 110 [FD 1.45 RT 6.53 FD 0.9 LT 1.82 RT 0.11 FD 0.16 RT (SIN REPCOUNT) FD 0.01]
REPEAT 80 [FD 0.95 RT 8.56 FD 0.5 LT 1.12 RT 0.09 FD 0.1 RT (SIN REPCOUNT) FD 0.012]

TASK: Compare canvas images and score them to guide the next evolution iteration.

SCORING (evaluate each image against the stated aesthetic taste):
- aestheticScore 1-10: how well this image embodies the aesthetic taste
- noveltyScore 0-10: how visually distinct from the parent (Image 0 is always 0)

Also provide suggestedImprovement: one specific, actionable Logo-level change for the next iteration.

Return ONLY valid JSON - no markdown, no backticks, no preamble:
{
  "evaluations": [
    {"imageNumber": 0, "aestheticScore": 7, "noveltyScore": 0, "reason": "one sentence"},
    {"imageNumber": 1, "aestheticScore": 8, "noveltyScore": 6, "reason": "one sentence"}
  ],
  "suggestedImprovement": "specific actionable Logo suggestion"
}

Iteration: 11

apiLog.systemPrompt:
You are an expert Logo turtle graphics generative artist.

TASK: Generate the requested number of visually distinct variants that are more aesthetically interesting.

INPUTS (in the user message):
- Aesthetic goal: user's taste preference
- Change magnitude: how drastically to evolve
- Base program: Logo code to evolve
- Critic feedback (if iterating): suggestions from previous round

OUTPUT FORMAT - CRITICAL: return ONLY variant blocks:
---VARIANT 1---
[complete Logo code]
---VARIANT 2---
[complete Logo code]

MAGNITUDE RULE: The STRICT MAGNITUDE CONSTRAINT in the user message is absolute. Magnitude 1: change exactly one number. Magnitude 2: change 2-3 numbers. Magnitude 3: change several parameters or add one simple command. Never add new procedures or structural changes at magnitudes 1-3.

COMPLETENESS RULE: Every variant MUST be a complete, runnable Logo program. Verify: (1) every [ has a matching ]; (2) every TO has a matching END; (3) the last line is complete. Do NOT include ANY reasoning, thinking, or commentary inside variant blocks.

Commands: FD BK RT LT PU PD HOME CS REPEAT n [ ] TO name :p (body) END MAKE "var val :var SETHEADING SETXY SETPC SETPENCOLOR SETPENSIZE SETWIDTH SETBG ARC CIRCLE DOT IF IFELSE WHILE FOR SIN COS SQRT RANDOM INT ABS MOD MAX MIN REPCOUNT OUTPUT STOP

SETPC/SETBG: number 0-15, named color (RED BLUE GREEN CYAN MAGENTA YELLOW WHITE BLACK ORANGE PURPLE GOLD TEAL LIME VIOLET INDIGO CRIMSON CORAL SKYBLUE MINT ROSE AMBER GRAY SILVER MAROON OLIVE), or [r g b] 0-255.
Canvas 400x400, origin center, North=up. FINITE programs. TO...END before calling. Under 60 lines.
